title: Off-Policy Corrected Reward Modeling for Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback

methodology.problem: Overoptimization (Goodharting) in RLHF where the reward model becomes inaccurate as the RL model diverges from the SFT model
methodology.proposed_solution: Off-Policy Corrected Reward Modeling (OCRM) using importance weighting to correct for distribution shift
methodology.approach: Multiple training stages, each consisting of training a corrected RM with importance weighting and then performing multiple policy updates
methodology.approximation: Instead of retraining the RM after each policy update, the method uses multiple policy updates between RM trainings

pipeline: Standard RLHF pipeline: SFT → Reward Modeling → Reinforcement Learning
OCRM pipeline: Train corrected RM with IW → Perform multiple policy updates → Repeat
main_findings: Standard RLHF methods provide inconsistent reward model parameter estimates after the first policy update, leading to non-convergence to optimal policy even with unlimited data and training iterations
advantages: OCRM iteratively improves RM accuracy on current policy samples and converges to higher final rewards without additional data
comparison: Compared to standard Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)-RLHF, OCRM avoids early stagnation and achieves better final rewards
